title: Bots Just Took Over the Internet. Cloudflare Is Building the Immune System.
subtitle: 57% of web traffic is now automated. Cloudflare's Precursor and Workers AI platform position NET as the invisible infrastructure for the agentic internet.
summary: Cloudflare's Precursor launch marks a turning point: bots now outnumber humans on the web. With $2.33B in TTM revenue (+33.5% YoY), $755M in FCF, and a platform play spanning bot defense, edge compute, and AI inference, NET is quietly remaking itself into the backbone of the agentic internet — but a 176x P/E leaves no room for error.

image.caption: Cloudflare edge network visualization — Precursor's behavioral validation runs across 330+ cities worldwide

videos.title: Cloudflare Agents Week Preview — Building for the Agentic AI Era

bodyHtml:
For the first time in internet history, bots outnumber humans. Automated traffic now accounts for 57% of all web requests, a threshold that Cloudflare's CTO Dane Knecht calls a 'seismic evolution' from an internet built for human clicks to a digital landscape dominated by AI agents. Cloudflare just launched Precursor, a behavioral validation engine that runs inside web browsers and monitors entire browsing sessions rather than checking a single CAPTCHA at the door. It replaces static, point-in-time defenses with continuous analysis of mouse movement, scrolling rhythm, typing cadence, and page visibility — building a behavioral fingerprint that automated agents simply cannot fake over time.

This is not an incremental product update. Precursor signals a fundamental rethinking of what Cloudflare is becoming. The company that built its name on CDN caching and DDoS mitigation is quietly remaking itself into the invisible infrastructure layer for what analysts are already calling the 'agentic internet' — a world where AI agents talk to APIs instead of rendering web pages, where machine-to-machine traffic dwarfs human browsing, and where the winning infrastructure provider is the one that sits closest to the data path.

Cloudflare's Workers AI platform is the other half of this thesis. It offers a unified inference layer that lets developers access any AI model through a single API, switching between models with one line of code. Over a million developers now build on Workers, and the platform handles everything from LLM inference on serverless GPUs to vector search, image generation, and durable execution. The strategic insight is simple: if you control the edge where AI agents run, and you control the security layer that validates their traffic, you own the stack between the model and the user.

The financial picture is strong but complicated. Cloudflare generated $2.33 billion in trailing twelve-month revenue, up 33.5% year over year, with a 73.3% gross margin that reflects the asset-light efficiency of its network. Free cash flow hit $755 million, a 13% margin that gives management ample room to invest. The company holds roughly $640 million in net cash and carries no meaningful debt. But on a GAAP basis, Cloudflare is still operating at a loss — operating margins sit at negative 9.7% — and the stock trades at 176 times earnings, a valuation that prices in years of perfection.

At $277.66, NET is less than 5% off its all-time high of $291. The stock is up 39.3% year to date and 43.8% over the past twelve months, reflecting the market's willingness to pay a premium for Cloudflare's narrative. Analysts are largely on board: 21 rate it a Buy, 8 say Hold, and only 4 recommend Sell. The mean price target sits at $254, but the high-end target is $322, with Citi upgrading to Buy and Scotiabank, Barclays, and TD Cowen all recently raising their targets to $300.

The competitive landscape reinforces the premium. Fastly trades at a $3.2 billion market cap on $653 million in revenue, growing at 19.8%. Akamai, the incumbent, commands $17.5 billion in market cap on $4.27 billion in revenue, growing at just 5.8%. Cloudflare, with roughly half of Akamai's revenue, trades at nearly six times Akamai's market cap. That spread is the pure growth premium, and sustaining it requires Cloudflare to keep delivering narrative-shifting product cycles like Precursor and Workers AI.

Cloudflare's infrastructure reach is its moat. The network spans 330+ cities, touching 95% of the global population within 50 milliseconds. Every new use case — AI inference at the edge, real-time bot detection, zero-trust network access — runs on that same global fabric, meaning unit economics improve with every additional service Cloudflare layers on top. RBC Capital Markets projects Cloudflare can exceed $5 billion in revenue before 2028 while maintaining a Rule-of-50 profile, a combination of growth and profitability that few enterprise tech companies achieve at scale.

The bull case is straightforward: in a world where AI agents are generating more code than developers, where bots outnumber humans on the open web, and where every enterprise needs to secure machine-to-machine traffic, Cloudflare's bet on being the connective tissue between all of it looks increasingly prescient. The risk is equally clear — a 176x earnings multiple leaves zero room for deceleration, and the 20% workforce reduction Cloudflare executed in May, while positioned as 'rightsizing for the agentic AI era,' also signals a company still figuring out how to translate growth into GAAP profitability.

Disclosure: The Signal holds no position in NET. Positions may change. This is not financial advice.
